pi_items.source: ArkCase documentation confirms case ID format follows pattern: year-prefix-sequential number. setup_task.sh:17 creates case with this title. Format 2024-RM-0047 aligns with ArkCase's configurable sequence format where RM=Records Management prefix and 0047=sequential number.

Legal case management documentation (PracticePanther, Everchron, California Courts Self-Help) and ArkCase documentation confirm 'Evidence' as a standard document folder category for legal case organization and management.

ArkCase documentation (https://www.arkcase.com/correspondence-management-2/) explicitly mentions 'Correspondence Management' as core functionality. Legal case file organization best practices confirm correspondence as a standard document category.
Legal case management documentation (PracticePanther legal file organization guide) confirms 'Legal Briefs' as a standard folder for organizing case-related legal documents, motions, and briefs.

Legal case management documentation (Everchron, California Courts Self-Help guide) confirms 'Witness Statements' as a standard document type in case files. ArkCase supports organizing witness statements as case documentation.
task.json:6 specifies 'Ensure the folder names match exactly (case-sensitive)'. Underscores used as separators follow standard folder naming conventions in ArkCase.

env.json:2 references 'arkcase_env@0.1'. ArkCase is a verified open-source case management platform documented at https://www.arkcase.com/ and https://github.com/ArkCase/ArkCase supporting complaint management, FOIA requests, and legal case workflows.

privileged_info_summary: This task uses synthetic test data. The case 'Records Management Folder Setup - 2024-RM-0047' is dynamically created during task setup via the ArkCase REST API. The case title follows ArkCase's documented sequence configuration format (year-prefix-sequential number). All four required folders—Evidence, Correspondence, Legal_Briefs, and Witness_Statements—are verified as standard document categories in legal case management, as confirmed by ArkCase's documentation (https://www.arkcase.com/), legal case management software providers (PracticePanther), legal file organization guides, and court administration resources (California Courts). The task operates on a development ArkCase instance deployed via Kubernetes with test credentials (arkcase-admin@dev.arkcase.com).
